questions: Phase 1 step 4: is there any legitimate consumer that needs the silent `EMPTY_FORM` fallback today, or can `useImageEditFormSafe` be deleted entirely once callsites are switched to the strict `useImageEditForm`?
Phase 2 step 7: prefer building a real `ImageEditProvider` around `InlineEditView`, or go pure-props? The former keeps the context story; the latter is a smaller diff. Which do you want?

Phase 3 step 10: should 'lightbox close' flush the prompt even if the user clicked away from the textarea without blurring (e.g., clicked the close button while still focused)? Assumed yes — please confirm.
Phase 4 step 12: does the repo already host custom ESLint rules, or should this be a grep-based script wired into lint? A quick pointer avoids a short detour.

success_criteria.criterion: `LoraSelectorModal.tsx` no longer contains `if (!isOpen) return null;` before the `<Dialog>` return
`PhaseConfigSelectorModal.tsx` no longer contains `if (!isOpen) return null;` before the `<Dialog>` return
`dialog.tsx` has a top-of-file comment documenting the no-early-return-with-open-Dialog rule
`useImageEditForm` exists and throws when its provider is missing; `useImageEditFormSafe` remains only for explicit opt-in callers (or is removed if none exist)
All existing vitest suites for media-lightbox, lora, PhaseConfigSelectorModal, and useAutoSaveSettings pass after each phase
Manual user smoke: can type in edit textarea and open model Select after using the LoRA selector (Phase 1 acceptance)
`EditModePanel.tsx` under ~150 lines after Phase 2; mode-specific bodies live in sibling files under `editModes/`
No IIFE-in-JSX blocks remain in `EditModePanel.tsx` after Phase 2
Exactly one source of truth for edit-panel state (props OR context, not both) after Phase 2 step 7
`useInpainting.ts` no longer wraps setters with `incomingSet ?? fallbackSet` and no longer holds fallback local state for these fields
Prompt field writes to Supabase only on blur / generate / lightbox close; slider and toggle fields remain debounced
`editVersionRef`, `incrementEditVersion`, and the 'newer edits during save' guard are removed from `useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts`
`useAutoSaveSettings` entity-change transitions go through a single reducer; no render-phase `setSettings` or ref mutations remain across the three previous locations
A lint rule or documented check fires on a fixture that contains `if (!open) return null;` immediately before a `<Dialog>` return
`reigh-app/CLAUDE.md` documents the no-op-default-context antipattern in a section that is discoverable during review
Phase 1 ships as a standalone PR and is merged before Phase 2 work begins
No new `.md` files created (CLAUDE.md edit only)

assumptions: Base UI `<Dialog open>` renders nothing heavy when closed at the Portal level, so removing the early-return does not regress closed-state performance; if it does, `{isOpen && <DialogContent />}` is the fallback.
`useImageEditForm` (strict) will replace all four current `useImageEditFormSafe` callsites except possibly the test file, which is updated to wrap in a provider.
`InlineEditView` can reasonably host an `ImageEditProvider`; going with that approach in Phase 2 step 7 unless exploration shows otherwise.
'Lightbox close' is an existing unmount boundary that already triggers the `useDebouncedSettingsSave` cleanup effect, so flushing prompt-on-close is free once the prompt is tracked as pending.
Custom ESLint rules infra may not exist; fallback is a documented grep-based lint script. Final choice confirmed during implementation.
The Phase 3 reducer lives inside `useAutoSaveSettings` internals and does not change its public API or break consumer tests beyond helper-level updates.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
